name: Preparing Butters, Jams, Jellies, and Marmalades (2 of 2)
recipeInstructions: There are two basic methods for making jams and jellies. The standard method, which does not require added pectin, works best with fruits naturally high in pectin. The other method requires the use of commercial liquid or powdered pectin and is much quicker. Ensure you add the correct amount of commercial pectin to specific fruits as instructed on each package for uniform gelling. Overcooking may break down pectin and prevent proper gelling. Prepare one batch at a time, following the recipe instructions. Filling jellies into larger jars can result in soft products. Stir constantly while cooking to avoid burning. Recipes are designed for specific jar sizes.
